title: Moving White Flakes in Dog Fur: Understanding Walking Dandruff
meta_desc: Find out why your dog has moving white flakes in their fur. Learn about Walking Dandruff (Cheyletiella mites) and when to see a veterinarian.
content_html:
What are the moving white flakes in my dog's fur?

If you observe what appears to be dandruff moving independently across your dog's skin, it is likely a parasitic infestation caused by Cheyletiella mites. This condition is commonly known as walking dandruff. These mites are large enough to be seen with the naked eye and spend their entire life cycle on the host, feeding on the skin's surface scales.

Is this a veterinary emergency?

The urgency level for walking dandruff is Low. While this condition is highly contagious to other pets and can cause significant itching or skin irritation, it is not a life-threatening emergency. You should schedule a standard appointment with your veterinarian within the next few days to begin treatment and prevent the infestation from spreading further.

Why a photo helps with triage

Taking a clear, close-up photo or a short video of the moving flakes can be incredibly beneficial for your veterinary team. Because these mites can sometimes be elusive or hidden during a physical exam, a visual record of the movement helps the vet confirm the presence of live parasites quickly. This allows them to prepare the appropriate diagnostic tests, such as a skin scraping or tape test, before you even arrive at the clinic.

faqs.question: Can humans catch walking dandruff from their dogs?
faqs.answer: Yes, Cheyletiella mites are zoonotic, meaning they can temporarily move to humans and cause an itchy rash or small red bumps, though they cannot complete their life cycle on human skin.

faqs.question: How is walking dandruff treated?
faqs.answer: Treatment usually involves professional-grade topical or oral parasite preventatives prescribed by a vet, alongside thorough cleaning of the pet's environment and bedding.

faqs.question: Is walking dandruff the same as regular dandruff?
faqs.answer: No. Regular dandruff is simply dead skin cells (seborrhea), whereas walking dandruff is caused by live mites moving those skin cells around as they crawl.
